jokes.text: It was terrible. / It was just the wrong sort of crowd. / You can't call that a crowd. / Yeah, three's a crowd.
jokes.setup: Disagreement about whether their performance was good, leading to debate about the audience size
jokes.punchline: Bret uses the idiom 'three's a crowd' literally to defend calling three people a crowd
jokes.explanation: Classic Flight of the Conchords deadpan literalism. Bret earnestly defending their tiny audience with an idiom taken literally shows great character consistency.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: It was terrible.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: It was just the wrong sort of crowd.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: You can't call that a crowd.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Yeah, three's a crowd.

jokes.text: Your act is sensational... The idea of a pair of naive idiots from New Zealand... it's so simple, it's genius. You just pick an obscure backwards country that nobody knows anything about... very funny.
jokes.setup: Actor praising their 'act' as brilliant comedy
jokes.punchline: He thinks they're doing a character bit when they're actually from New Zealand
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-comedy that comments on the show's premise while creating awkward misunderstanding. Well-constructed irony.

dialogue.speaker: Ben/Actor
dialogue.line: Your act is sensational... The idea of a pair of naive idiots from New Zealand... it's so simple, it's genius. You just pick an obscure backwards country that nobody knows anything about... very funny.

jokes.text: So where are you guys from? The Julliard School of the Performing Arts? / We're from New Zealand.
jokes.setup: Actor assumes they're trained performers from prestigious school
jokes.punchline: Bret's matter-of-fact delivery that they're actually from New Zealand
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response that maintains the awkward misunderstanding. Bret's innocent honesty against the actor's assumptions.

dialogue.speaker: Ben/Actor
dialogue.line: So where are you guys from? The Julliard School of the Performing Arts?

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: We're from New Zealand.

jokes.text: Lord of the Rings, Frodo. The ring. Anyway, don't let me bore you with my knowledge of New Zealand.
jokes.setup: Actor trying to show off his knowledge of New Zealand
jokes.punchline: His 'extensive knowledge' is just naming obvious Lord of the Rings references
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing American ignorance about New Zealand. The false expertise is well-executed.

dialogue.speaker: Ben/Actor
dialogue.line: Lord of the Rings, Frodo. The ring. Anyway, don't let me bore you with my knowledge of New Zealand.

jokes.text: This says 'Dry Cleaners.' / The other side. Written in pencil.
jokes.setup: Actor hands them his professional business card
jokes.punchline: It's a dry cleaner business card with his acting info scribbled on the back
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag that establishes the actor's amateur status. Functional but not particularly clever.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: This says 'Dry Cleaners.'

dialogue.speaker: Ben/Actor
dialogue.line: The other side. Written in pencil.

jokes.text: I tried to talk to a guy but he told me to shut up 'cause he was reading the paper. / Oh, you don't disturb people reading the paper, Jemaine. Could be big news he was getting into.
jokes.setup: Jemaine complaining about poor audience interaction
jokes.punchline: Bret seriously defending the audience member's right to read during their performance
jokes.explanation: Perfect character comedy - Bret's earnest defense of obviously rude behavior shows his naive optimism beautifully.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: I tried to talk to a guy but he told me to shut up 'cause he was reading the paper.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Oh, you don't disturb people reading the paper, Jemaine. Could be big news he was getting into.

jokes.text: What about if we give out some free pencils? / No, you're not in New Zealand now, Bret. / I know, but we gave them out, remember that? We got a box of 20 pencils... and that night... 20 people.
jokes.setup: Bret suggesting promotional strategies
jokes.punchline: His evidence that pencils work is hilariously literal - exactly 20 pencils brought exactly 20 people
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist logic that perfectly captures Bret's naive understanding of cause and effect. Great character-specific humor.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: What about if we give out some free pencils?

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: No, you're not in New Zealand now, Bret.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: I know, but we gave them out, remember that? We got a box of 20 pencils... and that night... 20 people.

jokes.setup: Sweet song about Murray's birthday party
jokes.punchline: They made a cake celebrating the wrong age
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment within an otherwise sweet song. The casual mention of their mistake adds realistic awkwardness.

dialogue.speaker: Bret, Jemaine
dialogue.line: We brought you a cake in the shape / Of a four and a three / 'Cause we all thought you were 43

dialogue.line: I couldn't even get that right.

jokes.explanation: Sets up character for later callback. The repetitive dialogue shows amateur acting while being somewhat quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Ben/Actor
dialogue.line: Hey, what... what's with all the commotion? What's this commotion? Explain to me what's with the commotion!

jokes.text: Hey, watch out, you flamin' drongo! Don't be such a flamin' bloody drongo!

jokes.punchline: Terrible Australian stereotype accent instead of New Zealand
jokes.explanation: Good cringe showing American confusion between Australia and New Zealand. Predictable but effective.

dialogue.speaker: Ben/Actor
dialogue.line: Hey, watch out, you flamin' drongo! Don't be such a flamin' bloody drongo!

jokes.text: Could you do it in your normal voice, maybe? / No. That's not acting.

dialogue.speaker: Ben/Actor
